abstraction: Open with the lifestyle the property enables (morning coffee on the terrace), describe the space (beds/baths/sqft), close with an invitation to view.
Lead with the single most compelling feature, layer in 3-4 supporting details, close with neighborhood/location context.
Identify the buyer pain (tired of renting, need space for family), present the property as the answer, end with concrete proof (schools, walk score, recent upgrades).
Lead with investment thesis (cap rate, rental yield), present the numbers plainly, close with future upside (development, appreciation trend).

structure: before/after renovation + feature walkthrough + move-in ready
abstraction: Contrast original state with current renovated state, walk through key upgrades room by room, close with move-in-ready reassurance.
Open with sensory-rich description (light, space, warmth), flow room-by-room, close with community and neighbors.
Urgent headline (rare find, won't last), state one unique differentiator, direct call-to-action to schedule viewing.
Tell a family-centric story (kids in the yard, school drop-off), list practical features, close with safety and community amenities.
